utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final coverage plan: it includes all five top-level items with corresponding sections. Next, verify each is fully specified with costs and required details: 1) health_coverage: explicitly states physical and mental health insurance with costs (€100/month and €60/month) and a total (€160/month) plus some coverage details. Fully specified. 2) property_coverage: includes home/contents insurance with cost (€55/month) and some coverage details, plus a total. Fully specified. 3) travel_coverage: includes travel insurance with cost (€55/month) and some coverage details, plus a total. Fully specified. 4) additional_services: includes enhancement services with costs? It lists bundle_discount, compliance_certification, effective_date, but does not provide explicit euro costs for these services; the discount is a percentage but not a price line item. The format asks for 'any enhancement services with costs'—costs are not explicitly provided, so this may be considered not fully specified. 5) total_budget: provides monthly and annual cost breakdown, including changes and percentages. Fully specified. Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: Map each selected item name from the final coverage plan to the exact matching item name in the ground-truth ratings and return its numeric rating. The final plan includes: LifeFirst Standard Health Plan for physical health (maps to Primary Health Coverage > physical_health > LifeFirst Standard Plan, rating 9); HealthFirst Standard Mental Health for mental health (maps to Primary Health Coverage > mental_health > HealthFirst Standard Plan, rating 6); HomeFirst Standard Property Coverage (maps to Property Protection > home_content > HomeFirst Standard Coverage, rating 9); TravelFirst Standard Travel Plan (maps to Mobility Coverage > travel_insurance > TravelFirst Standard Plan, rating 9).
